themes.title: Forgeline — Industrial & Trade Supplier HTML Template
themes.oneliner: A premium website template that actually understands the trade — datasheets, photometry downloads, quote requests and next-day delivery messaging, not another generic "business theme".
themes.description:
Most templates make an electrical wholesaler look like a startup. Forgeline was
designed *from inside the industry*: the demo content is a believable UK
commercial lighting distributor, with product spec tables (IP ratings, lumen
output, efficacy), a downloads tab for datasheets and LDT/IES photometric
files, trade-account CTAs, volume-break pricing and a "send us your bill of
materials" quote flow.

- 8 pages: Home, About, Products (filterable grid), Single Product, Services, Blog, Article, Contact
- HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS — open index.html and it just works, no build step
- Rebrand in minutes: every colour and font is a CSS variable
- Real photography included; product images as clean technical renders
- Vanilla JS only: tabs, filters, gallery, accordion, working demo forms
- WCAG-AA accessibility patterns, tested at iPhone widths, no horizontal scroll anywhere
- Plain-English README covering setup, rebranding and going live

Perfect for: electrical wholesalers, lighting suppliers, manufacturers,
engineering firms, industrial distributors, builders' merchants.

themes.title: Callout — Electrician Website Template (local trades)
themes.oneliner: A complete website for a local electrician — built around what actually wins trade work: reviews, fixed-quote promises, photos of real jobs, and a phone number everywhere.
themes.description:
Five pages (Home, Services, Recent Work, About, Free Quote) with a fully
written demo company so you see a real trade business, not lorem ipsum: trust
strip with guarantees, 6 service cards with honest trade copy, project cards
with budgets, review cards ready for your Checkatrade quotes, an areas-covered
strip, and a quote form with job-type dropdown.

- HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS — open index.html and it just works, no build step
- Rebrand in minutes: every colour and font is a CSS variable
- Vanilla JS only, WCAG-AA accessibility patterns, tested at iPhone widths
- Plain-English README covering setup, rebranding and going live

themes.title: Sideline — Grassroots Junior Football Club Template
themes.oneliner: The first club website template built for how junior clubs actually work — 20+ teams, boys' and girls' pathways, volunteers and safeguarding, no "first team" your club doesn't have.
themes.description:
Junior clubs don't need a fixtures-and-league-table site — they need parents to
find the right team, register in five minutes, and trust the setup. Sideline
is organised around exactly that:

- A **teams directory** with 24 demo teams across U6–U16, filterable by Boys / Girls / Mixed, each card showing league, format (4v4 → 11v11), training night, coach and live space availability
- Registration page with a fees table, sibling discounts and hardship fund — plus a parents' FAQ that answers the questions you get every July
- A volunteers page, because the real shortage is coaches, not players
- Safeguarding built in structurally: welfare contact first on the contact page, no junior players named, photo-consent language modelled
- 8 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested

themes.title: Terrace — Football Club HTML Template (claret & sky)
themes.oneliner: A complete football club website in claret & sky (plus a 5-way colour switcher) — fixtures, results, league table, squad profiles, match reports and academy trials, styled for your colours out of the box.
themes.description:
Your club's website shouldn't look like a corporate brochure with a ball
photo. Terrace is a complete club site in **claret & sky (plus a 5-way colour switcher)** — the demo club
(Ravenshaw Rovers, est. 1921) wears your end of the colour spectrum so you can picture
your own badge on it immediately.

- **Match centre**: fixtures, results with scorelines and W/D/L badges, and a league table with your club's row highlighted — all plain HTML, edited by copy-paste
- **Live kick-off countdown** on the homepage (set one date attribute)
- **Squad grid** with position filter and player profile page (with a "sponsor this player" revenue CTA)
- Match report layout, club history/honours, academy trials form with parents' FAQ, contact with matchday directions
- A proper **club crest** included as a recolourable SVG — swap in your own badge whenever you're ready
- 9 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA patterns, tested at iPhone widths
- Every colour is a CSS variable — matching your exact club colours is a five-minute job, documented in the README

Perfect for: non-league and semi-pro clubs, community clubs, Sunday league
sides, club volunteers who got handed "the website job".

Can't see your colours? Terrace is the any-colour edition: five ready-made colourways ship in the box (claret, royal, scarlet, forest, black & amber) switchable with one line of HTML — plus a live preview page — and the README shows how to hit your exact colours with one CSS block.

themes.title: Boundary — Cricket Club HTML Template (green & gold)
themes.oneliner: A complete cricket club website — fixtures with proper scorelines, league table, squad averages, junior section and a first-ball countdown, in classic cricket green & gold.
themes.description:
Your club's website shouldn't look like a football template with the badge swapped. Boundary is built for how cricket clubs actually run — the demo club (Longmead CC, est. 1894, home at The Meadow) reads like a real village club, not lorem ipsum.

- **Match centre**: fixtures and results with real cricket scorelines (187/6 dec, 40-over formats), W/L/D/T badges, and a league table with your club's row highlighted — all plain HTML, edited by copy-paste
- **First-ball countdown** on the homepage (set one date attribute)
- **Squad grid** with batting and bowling roles, player pages with averages, and a "sponsor this player" revenue CTA
- Junior cricket page: All Stars and Dynamos age groups, coaching nights, parents' FAQ
- Match report layout, club history and honours, contact with ground directions
- 8 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA patterns, tested at iPhone widths
- Every colour is a CSS variable — matching your exact club colours is a five-minute job, documented in the README

Perfect for: village and league cricket clubs, junior sections, club volunteers who got handed "the website job".

themes.title: Callout — Skip Hire Website Template (local trades)
themes.oneliner: A complete website for a skip hire firm — built around what actually wins waste work: clear skip sizes and prices, same-day delivery messaging, permit handling, and a phone number everywhere.
themes.description:
Five pages (Home, Skips & Prices, Recent Jobs, About, Get a Skip) with a fully written demo company (Loadstar Skips, Doncaster) so you see a real waste business, not lorem ipsum: skip size cards from 4-yard midis to roll-on-roll-off, licensed-waste-carrier trust strip, recent jobs with sizes and prices, review cards, an areas-covered strip, and a booking form with skip-size and waste-type dropdowns plus prohibited-items notes.

- HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS — open index.html and it just works, no build step
- Rebrand in minutes: every colour and font is a CSS variable
- Vanilla JS only, WCAG-AA accessibility patterns, tested at iPhone widths
- Plain-English README covering setup, rebranding and going live

themes.title: Callout — Scaffolding Website Template (local trades)
themes.oneliner: A complete website for a scaffolding company — CISRS-carded credibility, TG20:21 compliance messaging, recent contracts and a free site-survey form, styled in steel and amber.
themes.description:
Five pages (Home, Services, Recent Contracts, About, Site Survey) with a fully written demo company (Vertex Scaffolding, Birmingham) so you see a real access firm, not lorem ipsum: residential and commercial scaffolding services, temporary roofs, edge protection and inspections, an accreditation trust strip (CISRS, CHAS, £10m public liability), contract case cards with durations, review cards from site managers, and a survey-request form.

- HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS — open index.html and it just works, no build step
- Rebrand in minutes: every colour and font is a CSS variable
- Vanilla JS only, WCAG-AA accessibility patterns, tested at iPhone widths
- Plain-English README covering setup, rebranding and going live

themes.title: Solace — Funeral Directors HTML Template
themes.oneliner: A dignified website for a family funeral directors — 24-hour contact first, plain guidance for families, transparent pricing, and a tone that never sells.
themes.description:
Most templates shout. Solace is quiet on purpose — designed for a family firm (the demo is Fairbourne & Sons, Harrogate, serving families since 1932) where the website's job is to reassure at the hardest moment.

- 24-hour phone line prominent on every page, with "what to do first" guidance written in plain, kind English
- Services explained honestly: traditional funerals, cremation, direct cremation, woodland burial, pre-paid plans, repatriation
- Transparent-pricing section aligned with the CMA's standardised price list requirement for UK funeral directors
- Testimonials page with believable, respectful words from families
- About page: four generations of history, the team, the chapel of rest
- 5 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested
- A muted dusk-and-brass palette, changeable via CSS variables in minutes

Perfect for: independent funeral directors, family firms, memorial services.

themes.title: Commons — Village Hall & Community Centre Template
themes.oneliner: A warm website for a village hall — hire rates and booking, a what's-on grid of weekly clubs, facilities and access details, run-by-volunteers friendly.
themes.description:
Thousands of village halls run on a volunteer with a spare evening — Commons is built for exactly that (the demo is Elmsworth Village Hall, a charity-run hall built 1928).

- **Hire the Hall** page: rooms with capacities and an hourly-rates table, resident and regular-club discounts, how booking and deposits work
- **What's On** grid: weekly clubs from pilates to short mat bowls, plus one-off events
- Facilities up front: capacity, kitchen, stage, parking, wheelchair access, hearing loop
- About page: the hall's history, the trustees, the charity number, how to volunteer
- Booking enquiry form with event type, date, room and numbers
- 5 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested
- Every colour is a CSS variable — five-minute rebrand, documented in the README

Perfect for: village halls, community centres, church halls, scout huts, parish councils.

themes.title: Swim — Fishing Lakes & Day-Ticket Fishery Template
themes.oneliner: A complete website for a day-ticket fishery — lakes and prices up front, catch reports, venue rules and records, in deep-water blue and reed green.
themes.description:
Anglers check three things before they travel: prices, stock and rules. Swim puts all three on the surface — the demo venue (Willow Pool Fisheries, Shropshire, three lakes on a family farm since 1987) reads like somewhere you'd actually fish.

- **Lakes & Prices**: each lake with its stock, pegs and character (runs water, silvers lake, specimen syndicate), plus a day/evening/season ticket price table
- **Catch Reports** page: recent catches with weights, baits and pegs, and a venue records list
- Day-ticket prices and opening times on the homepage, above the fold
- Rules summary written like a fishery actually writes them (barbless only, unhooking mats, no braid)
- About page: the farm's story, stocking policy, otter fencing, match bookings
- 5 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested
- Every colour is a CSS variable — five-minute rebrand, documented in the README

Perfect for: day-ticket fisheries, carp lakes, angling clubs, farm diversification ventures.

themes.title: Tryline — Rugby League Club HTML Template
themes.oneliner: A complete rugby league club website in deep maroon and amber — fixtures, league table with points difference, squad by position, juniors and match reports, with a gritty condensed-type design of its own.
themes.description:
Built for the community game — the demo club (Millbrook Raiders RLFC, est. 1923, The Foundry Ground, NCL Division Two) reads like a proper northern club, not a reskin.

- **Match centre**: rugby league scorelines, W/L/D badges, league table with points difference, all plain HTML edited by copy-paste
- **Squad grid** by position group (props to loose forwards) with player pages showing tries, goals and appearances
- Juniors page from tag rugby U8s to U16s with parents' FAQ
- Match report layout, club history, clubhouse news, contact with ground directions
- Its own design identity: Barlow Condensed display type, sharp corners, diagonal-stripe hero treatment
- 8 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested
- Every colour is a CSS variable — five-minute rebrand, documented in the README

themes.title: Greenside — Lawn Bowls Club HTML Template
themes.oneliner: A heritage-styled website for a bowls club — fixtures and rink results, membership and open days, new-bowler taster sessions, in bowling green and brass with readable serif elegance.
themes.description:
Thousands of UK bowls clubs, almost no templates made for them. Greenside's demo club (Avonford Bowls Club, est. 1907, two greens) is written for the actual audience: clear, welcoming, unhurried.

- Fixtures and results as bowls matches with rink scores, plus the league table
- **Members & Teams** page: Saturday league, midweek triples, ladies' team and the newcomers' roll-up
- **New Bowlers** page: free taster sessions, what to wear, equipment loaned, green etiquette
- Club history with county pennants, finals-day news, green fees and open days
- Heritage design of its own: Playfair Display serif, cream tints, double-rule borders, larger base text for readability
- 8 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested

themes.title: Southpaw — Boxing & Martial Arts Gym Template
themes.oneliner: A dark, punchy website for a boxing gym — class timetable, no-contract membership pricing, coach profiles and fighter stories, with Anton display type and fight-red accents.
themes.description:
The demo gym (Southside Boxing Academy, Salford, England Boxing affiliated) is written to get someone through the door for their first session.

- **Classes & Timetable**: fundamentals, sparring, juniors, ladies-only, boxfit and 1-to-1 pads — each with its weekly slot
- Membership pricing block: monthly, junior and drop-in rates, no contracts
- **Our Fighters** page: amateur bout results, show nights and a first-bout story
- The head coach's story and the railway-arch gym
- First-session booking form with experience-level dropdown
- Its own design identity: near-black sections, oversized Anton headings, red slash accents
- 5 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested

themes.title: Clearview — Window Cleaning Business Template
themes.oneliner: A light, airy website built around the window-cleaning round — 'your street every four weeks', text-the-night-before reminders, gutter and conservatory add-ons, join-the-round form.
themes.description:
Window cleaning runs on rounds, and Clearview is the rare template that understands that. The demo business (Clearview Window Cleaning, Norwich) sells reliability, not one-off jobs.

- Homepage built around the recurring round: 4- or 8-weekly visits, reminder texts, pay-online-after-each-clean
- Services: domestic rounds, conservatory roofs, gutter clearing with camera proof, fascias, solar panels, storefronts
- Round areas with postcodes, price guide by property size, reviews about turning up when promised
- Join-the-round form with frequency and property-type dropdowns
- Its own design identity: sky gradients, Nunito Sans, pill buttons, soft shadows — clean-glass airy
- 5 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested

themes.title: Vanguard — Man & Van / Removals Template
themes.oneliner: A reassuring website for a removals firm — ballpark prices up front, goods-in-transit insurance messaging, WhatsApp video surveys, and a quote form that asks the right questions.
themes.description:
Moving house is stressful; Vanguard's job is to look organised. The demo firm (Vanguard Removals, Reading, est. 2015) leads with the two things customers want: a price and proof you're insured.

- Ballpark pricing up front: man & van, 2-man crew and full house moves
- Services: single items and marketplace pickups, flat/house moves, office moves, packing with materials, student moves
- **Recent Moves** stories including a piano and a last-minute landlord clear-out
- Quote form with move type, from/to postcodes, date and lift access — plus the WhatsApp video-survey explainer
- Its own design identity: navy and go-green, Chivo type, dashed route-line motifs, arrow buttons
- 5 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested

themes.title: Showroom — Mobile Car Valeting & Detailing Template
themes.oneliner: A gloss-black website for a mobile valeter — package prices from mini valet to ceramic coating, transformation stories, and a booking form that asks about driveways, in cyan-edged automotive style.
themes.description:
Detailing customers buy the finish — Showroom looks like one. The demo business (Showroom Mobile Valeting, Milton Keynes) is a specialist gone two-van.

- Package pricing up front: mini valet, full valet, enhancement detail, ceramic coating — each itemised line by line
- Add-ons: pet hair, headlight restoration, engine bay, interior ozone
- **Recent Details**: transformation stories from neglected family SUV to show-prep classic
- Booking form with package, vehicle size and parking situation
- Its own design identity: gloss-black bands with a diagonal sheen, cyan edge-glow cards, Saira type, spec-plate price tags
- 5 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested

themes.title: Wagtail — Dog Grooming Salon Template
themes.oneliner: A warm, rounded website for a dog groomer — prices by size and coat, one-dog-at-a-time reassurance for nervous dogs, fresh-trim gallery stories, salon plus mobile van.
themes.description:
Owners choose groomers on trust. Wagtail's demo business (Wagtail Dog Grooming, Chester, City & Guilds Level 3) leads with calm: one dog at a time, no cages, nervous dogs welcome.

- Services priced by dog size: full groom, bath & tidy, puppy introductions, hand stripping, de-shedding, nails & teeth
- **Fresh Trims** gallery stories — a matted rescue cockapoo, a show-standard schnauzer, a first puppy groom
- Booking form with breed, size, coat condition and first-visit flag
- The groomer's story, the calm salon philosophy, the mobile van's coverage
- Its own design identity: cocoa and apricot, Quicksand type, extra-round corners, paw-print list bullets
- 5 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested

themes.title: Hedgerow — Farm Shop & Butchery HTML Template
themes.oneliner: A rustic-premium website for a farm shop — filterable shop sections from butchery to hampers, provenance-rich product pages, what's-in-season strips and farm notes, in soil brown and hedgerow green.
themes.description:
Farm shops sell provenance, and Hedgerow is built to show it. The demo (Hedgerow Farm Shop, Peak District, farming since 1946) makes you hungry — which is the job.

- **The Shop**: filterable grid across butchery, greengrocery, dairy & eggs, bakery, pantry and hampers, with honest prices
- Product page built for provenance: breed, hanging time and what's in the box instead of tech specs
- Veg box delivery rounds, hampers and corporate gifts, counter orders, café corner
- Farm notes blog: lambing diary, why asparagus season is short
- Opening hours prominent everywhere; four generations of story without buzzword soup
- Its own design identity: Fraunces serif, cream paper tints, kraft-label price tags, offset hand-drawn borders
- 8 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested

themes.title: Wayside — Caravan & Camping Site Template
themes.oneliner: A fresh-air website for a caravan and camping site — pitch types and seasonal prices up front, facilities and site rules in plain English, quiet-site policy, walks from the gate.
themes.description:
Campers compare pitches, prices and shower blocks — Wayside answers all three above the fold. The demo site (Wayside Meadow, North Yorkshire coast, 60 pitches, March–October) is family-run and proudly quiet.

- **Pitches & Prices**: grass, electric hook-up, hardstanding and seasonal pitches, each with size, amps and awning rules, priced by season
- Facilities strip: heated shower block, laundry, dog walk field, playground, shop with camping gas
- Arrival times, deposits and cancellation explained like a human wrote them
- The site & around: sea views, Cleveland Way walks, Whitby 15 minutes, gates locked 10pm
- Booking enquiry form with pitch type, dates, party size and dog flag — plus the narrow-lane sat-nav warning
- Its own design identity: sea teal and campfire orange, Bitter slab type, contour-pattern hero bands, badge price tags
- 5 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested

themes.title: Greenlight — Driving Instructor Website Template
themes.oneliner: An optimistic website for a driving school — lesson and block prices up front, first-time pass wall, calm-teaching promise, manual and automatic, with a traffic-light design motif.
themes.description:
Learners (and their parents) choose on trust and price. Greenlight's demo (Greenlight Driving School, Leicester, DVSA ADI Grade A) leads with both.

- Pricing up front: hourly, block-of-ten and intensive courses; first-time pass rate stat
- Services: manual and automatic lessons, intensives, refreshers and motorway confidence, Pass Plus, theory support
- **Recent Passes** wall: first-timers, a nervous re-taker, a 17th-birthday pass
- Booking form with transmission and postcode; test centres named; theory-to-test timeline explainer
- The instructor's story and a no-shouting-ever teaching philosophy
- Its own design identity: steady blue and green-light green, Rubik type, traffic-light kicker dots, L-plate stat badges
- 5 pages, HTML + compiled Tailwind CSS, no build step, vanilla JS, WCAG-AA, iPhone-tested

themes.title: Anvil — Premium Conversion-First Shopify Theme
themes.oneliner: An original OS 2.0 Shopify theme with the best-seller feature set built in: mega menu with images, quick-view, slide-out AJAX cart with free-shipping bar, sticky add-to-cart, promo tiles and 18+ configurable sections — no apps needed.
themes.description:
Anvil is an original, from-scratch Shopify OS 2.0 theme that builds in the features stores usually pay monthly apps for:

- **Mega menu** with image blocks, predictive-search-ready header, cart bubble
- **Quick-view** product modals straight from collection grids
- **Slide-out AJAX cart** with a free-shipping progress bar
- **Sticky add-to-cart** bar on product pages, dynamic checkout buttons, variant-aware pricing with sale badges
- Promo tiles, countdown banner, testimonials, FAQ accordion, logo list, multicolumn value props — 18+ sections, all JSON-configurable in the theme editor
- Storefront filtering and sorting on collections, related products, blog and article templates
- Speed-first: vanilla JavaScript, no jQuery, no frameworks; accessible drawers and modals (focus traps, Escape to close, reduced-motion respected)
- Colours, typography, radius and layout width all editable in theme settings — no code
- 100% original code (not a Dawn fork) — licensed for sale on any marketplace

Install: Online Store → Themes → Upload zip. Full customisation guide in the README.
